meta_title: For Founders: testing automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Testing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your lack of portfolio. Learn how to use testing automation with Antigravity and Claude Code to build credible, production-ready SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: You don't need a massive portfolio to win; you need proof of reliability. By implementing automated testing, you demonstrate professional engineering standards that convert skeptical leads into paying customers. Use Antigravity to architect your suite and Claude Code to execute, turning your MVP into a high-trust, revenue-generating asset.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is that buyers don't care about your ideas—they care about your stability. When you lack a portfolio, every bug is a nail in your product's coffin. You are currently fighting a war for trust, and without a robust testing layer, you are losing before you even launch.

But here is the secret: you don't need years of experience to engineer trust. You need to automate it. By shifting your workflow to include automated testing, you move from 'hobbyist builder' to 'technical founder.' This guide shows you how to leverage modern AI agents to build a bulletproof testing foundation that signals quality to every potential investor and user.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path
steps.description: Identify the one user flow that generates revenue. Testing everything is a trap; test what keeps the lights on.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and identify the 'happy path' critical for user onboarding and billing. Create a test strategy document prioritizing these core functions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify the critical path in this repository and output a plan for unit testing'.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Test Harness
steps.description: Set up a lightweight, automated environment so you can run tests with a single command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Jest and Playwright testing environment for this project. Ensure all configuration files are optimized for CI/CD.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a testing framework for this project and configure a base test file'.

steps.title: Automate Data Validation
steps.description: Ensure your database interactions are predictable, preventing the most common cause of SaaS downtime.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write integration tests for the user authentication and database insertion logic to ensure data integrity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create integration tests for the current database schema operations'.

steps.title: Simulate User Friction
steps.description: Use end-to-end tests to simulate real user behavior and catch edge cases that break your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Playwright script that simulates a user signing up and completing a checkout flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a full E2E test suite for the registration and checkout flow'.

steps.title: Establish Continuous Verification
steps.description: Hook your tests into your deployment pipeline so you never ship a broken feature again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the entire test suite on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a CI workflow file to run tests on every commit'.

pro_tips: Test for the 'Empty State'—most founders forget that the most common user state is an empty database.
Treat your test suite as your best documentation; it explains exactly how your app should behave.
Use mutation testing tools to verify your tests are actually catching bugs, not just passing.
Automate your regression tests first; never let a bug you have already fixed return to production.
Keep test execution time under 60 seconds; if it takes longer, developers will stop running them.
content.next_steps: Once your test suite is live, share your GitHub repository link with potential leads. Showing a 'passing' build status on your README is a powerful way to demonstrate technical maturity.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for scale. Your future users are waiting for a product that actually works. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
